exercises.name: Cable Front Raise
exercises.notes: Setup: Attach a straight bar or single handle to the low pulley.
instructions: Setup: Attach a straight bar or single handle to the low pulley. Stand facing away from or slightly offset to the machine with feet hip‑width apart and a slight bend in the knees (standing, not kneeling or prone).

Grip the bar/handle with an overhand grip (palms down) approximately shoulder‑width (or hold a single attachment with both hands centered). Keep wrists neutral and arms extended with a small bend at the elbows.
Brace your core and maintain an upright torso (avoid leaning back or using hip drive). This is a standing, not seated, position.

Slowly raise the bar/handle straight in front of you with control until your arms are roughly parallel to the floor (shoulder height). Keep the motion driven by the shoulders—do not swing or jerk the weight.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders engaged, then inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the start position under control.
Repeat for the desired repetitions. Use a weight that allows strict form and avoid excessive momentum or back extension.
exercises.description: Standing cable front raise is a unilateral or two‑handed movement that lifts a low‑pulley handle or bar straight in front of the body to shoulder height, primarily working the anterior delts. It uses the cable’s constant tension and requires core stabilization to control the lift.

exercises.name: Lever Military Press (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Load plates and adjust the seat so the handles are at roughly shoulder height when seated upright.
instructions: Load plates and adjust the seat so the handles are at roughly shoulder height when seated upright.
Sit with your back flat against the pad, feet flat on the floor, and grasp the handles with a firm overhand/neutral grip at shoulder level.
Brace your core and keep your chest up and head in a neutral position throughout the movement.
Unlock or release the safety/starting latch (if present) to allow the lever to move freely.
Exhale and press the handles upward in a controlled path until your arms are almost fully extended overhead without hyperextending the elbows.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining shoulder stability, then inhale and lower the lever under control back to the starting position at shoulder height.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, then return the lever to the safety/re-rack before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: Seated plate-loaded lever military press performed on a shoulder press machine; you press the lever handles overhead from shoulder height to target the delts. The movement also engages the triceps and upper trapezius for extension and stabilization.

exercises.name: Cable Single Arm Low Fly
exercises.notes: Step 1: Set a single handle on a low cable pulley (near floor level).
instructions: Step 1: Set a single handle on a low cable pulley (near floor level). Stand with the cable side to your non-working side so the line of pull is across your body.
Step 2: Take a staggered or shoulder-width stance with a slight forward lean at the hips; brace your core and keep your torso tall and stable throughout the movement.
Step 3: Grasp the handle with a neutral or slightly supinated grip and raise your arm so the elbow is slightly bent (soft elbow) and aligned roughly with the lower chest at the start.

Step 4: Exhale and pull the handle upward and across your body in a wide arc, leading with the elbow and squeezing the pectoral at the end range where your hand finishes near the midline of the chest.
Step 5: Avoid excessive torso rotation or shrugging; keep the shoulder down and allow the scapula to protract slightly as you reach across to maximize chest contraction.
Step 6: Inhale and slowly return the handle along the same controlled path to the starting position, maintaining tension on the cable and control of the shoulder.
Step 7: Perform the desired reps, then switch sides; use a weight that allows full control and a smooth arc without jerking.
exercises.description: A unilateral cable fly performed with the pulley set low; the handle is pulled upward and across the body to horizontally adduct and internally rotate the shoulder, isolating the chest. This variation emphasizes the lower and inner portion of the pectoral while demanding scapular stability.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rear Delt Raise
exercises.notes: Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is around 30–45 degrees (or nearly parallel to the floor) with a soft bend in the knees; keep the spine neutral and neck aligned.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip.
Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is around 30–45 degrees (or nearly parallel to the floor) with a soft bend in the knees; keep the spine neutral and neck aligned.
Let the dumbbells hang under your shoulders with elbows slightly bent (not locked). Brace your core to prevent swinging.

Exhale and raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, leading with the elbows and keeping the slight elbow bend, until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor or in line with the shoulders.
Pause and squeeze the rear delts and upper back at the top for 1 second, then inhale and lower the weights slowly under control back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired reps, avoiding momentum—keep the movement slow and controlled and maintain the hip hinge throughout.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell raise that isolates the rear (posterior) deltoids by lifting the arms out to the sides while the torso is hinged forward. Performed with a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo to minimize momentum and emphasize the rear delts and upper back.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rear Delt Raise
exercises.notes: Stand feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip.
instructions: Stand feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip.
Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor, keep a neutral spine and knees slightly bent (bent-over position).
Let your arms hang straight down from the shoulders with a slight bend in the elbows; maintain a braced core and avoid rounding the back.
Keeping the elbows slightly bent, raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the ground (level with the shoulders).
Squeeze the rear delts and shoulder blades together at the top for a brief pause, avoid shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the start under control, resisting any momentum; repeat for desired reps.
Keep the movement strict: use lighter weight if you must swing the torso or bend the elbows excessively, and breathe out on the lift and in on the lower.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell raise that isolates the rear (posterior) deltoids by lifting the arms out to the sides while the torso is hinged forward. It emphasizes shoulder horizontal abduction and scapular retraction with a controlled tempo.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Clean and Press
exercises.notes: Start standing with feet about hip-width to slightly wider, barbell over mid-foot; grip the bar just outside the knees with a double overhand or hook grip and chest up.
instructions: Start standing with feet about hip-width to slightly wider, barbell over mid-foot; grip the bar just outside the knees with a double overhand or hook grip and chest up.
Hinge at the hips and bend the knees so your hips are low, back flat, shoulders slightly in front of the bar and eyes forward (front of a squat rack/sightline).
Initiate the lift by driving through the heels and extending the hips and knees explosively while keeping the bar close to your body; shrug forcefully at full extension.

As you shrug, pull the elbows high and outside to accelerate the bar upward and quickly drop under the bar into a partial front-rack squat to 'catch' the bar on your shoulders (front rack position) with elbows high.
Stand up tall from the catch, fully extending the hips and knees to a stable upright front-rack position with the bar resting on the shoulders/clavicles and elbows forward.

From the front-rack, press the bar overhead by driving through the legs slightly (if using a push press) or strictly extend the arms (strict press), finishing with arms locked overhead and head slightly forward under the bar.

Lower the bar back to the front-rack in a controlled manner, then return the bar to the floor by hinging at the hips and bending the knees (reset) or carefully drop into a controlled descent if using appropriate technique and environment.
Maintain a braced core and neutral spine throughout, keep the bar path close to the body, and breathe/exhale during the press portion; reset between reps if necessary to preserve form.
exercises.description: A compound Olympic-style lift that combines a barbell clean (explosive hip extension and pull to bring the bar to the front rack) with a strict or push overhead press to finish. Trains shoulder pressing strength while recruiting legs, posterior chain, upper back and core for power and stability.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with elbows bent ~90°, palms facing forward or slightly inward and wrists neutral.
Inhale, keep the spine neutral and shoulders down away from the ears.
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled motion until the arms are nearly fully extended overhead (avoid locking the elbows).
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts and keeping the torso stable.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height with control to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a steady tempo and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Use a lighter weight or a spotter if form breaks down or if pressing heavy loads.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell shoulder press is a compound pressing movement performed from a seated, back‑supported position that primarily develops the delts while also engaging the triceps and upper back for stabilization.

exercises.name: Cable Neutral grip Chest Press
exercises.notes: Set the cable pulleys to a low position (near the floor) on both sides and attach neutral D‑handles; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set the cable pulleys to a low position (near the floor) on both sides and attach neutral D‑handles; select an appropriate weight.
Lie supine on a flat bench placed so the cables run directly toward your chest; plant feet flat on the floor and maintain a small natural arch in the lower back.
Grasp one handle in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) and bring the handles to chest level with elbows bent and approximately 45 degrees from your torso.
Retract and stabilize your shoulder blades (pinch them together and down) to protect the shoulders and create a solid chest press base.
Exhale and press both handles forward in a controlled line until your arms are nearly extended, keeping a slight bend in the elbows and palms facing each other.
Pause briefly at the finish while maintaining tension in the pectorals and stable scapulae; avoid locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly return the handles back to the starting position at chest level with controlled motion, resisting the cables.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while maintaining scapular stability, neutral spine, and even tempo throughout the set.
exercises.description: A lying cable press performed with neutral (palms facing each other) grips that targets the chest while using cables to provide continuous tension. The movement presses the handles forward from the chest to near full arm extension while maintaining a stable torso on a flat bench.

exercises.notes: Load plates and adjust the seat so the handles are at roughly shoulder height when seated upright.

Sit with your back flat against the pad, feet flat on the floor, and grasp the handles with a firm overhand/neutral grip at shoulder level.

exercises.name: Barbell Wide Grip Upright Row
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell held in front of your thighs with a wide overhand grip (hands wider than shoulder width).
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell held in front of your thighs with a wide overhand grip (hands wider than shoulder width).
Brace your core, keep your chest up and a slight bend in the knees; let the bar hang at arm's length with wrists neutral.
Initiate the movement by driving your elbows up and out to the sides, pulling the bar straight up toward the upper chest/collarbone while keeping the bar close to the body.
Lead with the elbows so they finish higher than the hands; stop when the bar reaches about upper-chest level or when elbows are roughly shoulder height.
Pause briefly at the top, squeezing the delts and traps, then lower the bar under control back to the starting position.
Maintain an upright torso, avoid excessive torso lean or using momentum, and do not let the wrists excessively bend or the shoulders internally rotate to reduce risk of impingement.
exercises.description: A standing barbell movement performed with a wide overhand grip that elevates the elbows out and up to target the shoulder (deltoid) muscles with strong involvement of the traps. The wide grip emphasizes the lateral delts and reduces internal rotation at the shoulder.

exercises.name: Cable One Arm Front Raise
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.

Stand upright (neutral spine) facing away from the machine with feet hip-width apart or in a slight staggered stance for stability; grasp the handle with the working hand using an overhand grip (palm facing down) and let the arm hang in front of your thigh with a small bend in the elbow.
Brace your abs, keep your chest tall and shoulders down, and hold the non-working hand on your hip or at your side to prevent torso movement.
Exhale and raise the working arm straight forward in a controlled arc until the hand reaches shoulder height (arm roughly parallel to the floor); keep the elbow slightly bent and the wrist neutral.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts; avoid shrugging the shoulder or leaning back.
Inhale and slowly lower the arm back to the starting position under control, resisting the cable.
Perform the desired reps on one side, then switch to the other arm. Maintain strict form—no swinging or using body momentum.
exercises.description: A unilateral cable front raise that isolates the anterior deltoid by lifting a single arm straight in front of the body from a low pulley. It provides constant tension through the range of motion and requires core stabilization to prevent torso movement.

exercises.notes: Stand feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip.
